title: Penpot: Your Open-Source Design Studio, Built for Developers
article:
Have you ever wished for a design tool that truly understands developers and respects your need for control? Penpot is here to make that wish a reality. This incredible open-source platform is engineered for teams that build digital products at scale, offering a fresh perspective on how design and development can seamlessly integrate.

Penpot's standout feature is its commitment to giving you full ownership over your design infrastructure. Unlike many proprietary tools, Penpot is built from the ground up to support self-hosting. This means your team can deploy it directly onto your own servers, ensuring strict compliance with governance requirements and complete control over your data. Whether you choose to use it in the browser or host it yourself, you're always in command.

What truly sets Penpot apart is its embrace of open standards. It works natively with SVG, CSS, HTML, and JSON, which are the very languages developers use every day. This fundamental alignment bridges the gap between design and code, making the handoff process smoother than ever. Designers create, and developers see the output in familiar, actionable formats, drastically reducing translation errors and speeding up product delivery.

Collaboration is at the heart of Penpot. It offers robust real-time collaborative capabilities, allowing multiple team members to work on the same design files simultaneously. This fosters a more dynamic and iterative design process, helping teams scale their efforts and bring design closer to the product development cycle.

Developers will especially appreciate how Penpot treats design as code. This approach enables a direct translation from visual concepts to functional implementation. Furthermore, Penpot provides best-in-class native Design Tokens. These tokens act as a single source of truth, ensuring consistency across your entire design system and making it incredibly easy to manage complex UI elements and themes. This means less manual work for developers and more time spent building.

The platform extends its power with the MCP server, which facilitates multi-directional workflows between design and code. Coupled with a powerful open API and a flexible plugin system, Penpot is not just a tool; it's a customizable ecosystem. You can integrate it with your existing toolchain, automate tasks, and extend its functionality to fit your team's unique needs, truly empowering developers to shape their design environment.
key_takeaways: Penpot offers full ownership and self-hosting capabilities for design infrastructure, ensuring data control and compliance.
It champions open standards like SVG, CSS, HTML, and JSON, bridging the design-code gap for smoother developer handoffs.
Features real-time collaboration and treats design as code, making it highly developer-friendly and speeding up product delivery.
Native Design Tokens ensure consistency and simplify design system management by providing a single source of truth.
Extensible with an open API, plugin system, and MCP server for advanced, customizable workflows and integrations.
readme_summary: Penpot is an open-source design and prototyping platform that enables teams to collaborate on digital product development. It emphasizes full ownership of design infrastructure through self-hosting and works with open standards like SVG, CSS, and HTML, integrating design and code with features like Design Tokens and a powerful API.
description: Penpot: The open-source design tool for design and code collaboration
